blocks.body: A radical is simplified when: no perfect square factor remains under the radical, no fractions under the radical, no radicals in the denominator.
To simplify sqrt(n): find the largest perfect square factor of n. Split, take the square root of the perfect square part. Example: sqrt(72) = sqrt(36 x 2) = 6 sqrt(2).

blocks.label: Examples
blocks.body: sqrt(50) = sqrt(25 x 2) = 5 sqrt(2). sqrt(98) = 7 sqrt(2). sqrt(12) = 2 sqrt(3). sqrt(75) = 5 sqrt(3).
Rationalizing the denominator: multiply numerator and denominator by the radical in the denominator. Example: 3/sqrt(5) = 3 sqrt(5)/5.
Product rule: sqrt(a x b) = sqrt(a) x sqrt(b). Quotient rule: sqrt(a/b) = sqrt(a)/sqrt(b). These only work when a >= 0 and b > 0.
